stem: A agriculture study measures the yield per hectare (tonnes) for a sample of 20 farms. The sample mean is 304.4 tonnes with a sample standard deviation of 91.7 tonnes. However, 2 of the 20 observations appear to be outliers (values more than 2.5 standard deviations from the mean). If these outliers are removed, the remaining 18 observations have a mean of 289.7 tonnes and standard deviation of 59.7 tonnes. Estimate the true population mean and provide a 95% confidence interval that accounts for the uncertainty about whether outliers should be included.

ground_truth.answer: With all data: mean = 304.4, 95% CI = [261.5, 347.3]. Without outliers: mean = 289.7, 95% CI = [261.6, 317.8]. Combined interval accounting for analytic uncertainty: [261.5, 347.3]. Point estimate: 297.0 tonnes.

solution_steps: The outlier treatment choice creates structural uncertainty. Combined interval: [261.5, 347.3].
common_failure_modes: Using only the full sample without considering outlier impact
Removing outliers without reporting both analyses
Not recognizing that the choice of analysis method is itself a source of uncertainty
